description: Master merger regulatory approval reference file. Used to scope applicable approvals from deal documents and track progress to closing.

usage.scoping_phase: For each approval, use scoping.trigger_question and scoping.signals_in_document to ask whether it applies to this deal. Look for signals in the merger agreement, proxy statement, or other deal documents.
usage.monitoring_phase: Once an approval is confirmed applicable, use monitoring.key_questions to query subsequent documents (press releases, SEC filings, regulatory dockets, news) for status updates.
usage.deal_tracker: The deal_tracker_fields list defines the fields to populate in a deal-level tracker JSON for each applicable approval.

approvals.name: HSR Filing (Hart-Scott-Rodino)
approvals.description: Premerger notification required under 15 U.S.C. § 18a if size-of-transaction (>$119.5M indexed) and size-of-person thresholds are met. Initiates a 30-day waiting period (15 days for cash tender offers).

scoping.trigger_question: Do the parties meet the HSR size-of-transaction threshold (currently $119.5M, indexed annually) and at least one size-of-person threshold? Is any HSR exemption claimed (e.g., foreign issuer, certain real estate, ordinary course)?
signals_in_document: Regulatory approval covenants referencing HSR
Outside date set 12–18 months out (signals expectation of extended review)
Transaction value disclosed in recitals — compare to current HSR threshold
Any claim of HSR exemption in representations or disclosure schedules
Reference to DOJ or FTC as the reviewing agency

threshold_details.note: Thresholds indexed annually by FTC; verify current year threshold

key_questions: Has the HSR notification been filed with DOJ/FTC?
What is the waiting period expiration date?
Has a Second Request been issued? If so, what is the substantial compliance deadline?
Has either agency granted early termination?
Has the waiting period been terminated or expired?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 30 days initial waiting period; Second Request adds 30 days after substantial compliance
monitoring.closing_trigger: Waiting period expired or terminated without challenge, or Second Request complied with and additional period elapsed

approvals.name: DOJ Antitrust Division Clearance
approvals.authority: US Department of Justice — Antitrust Division
approvals.description: DOJ substantive antitrust review. DOJ and FTC divide merger review jurisdiction; DOJ typically reviews tech, defense, telecom, banking, and agriculture. May result in clearance letter, consent decree, or litigation.

scoping.trigger_question: Is DOJ the designated reviewing agency (vs. FTC)? What are the horizontal product and geographic market overlaps? Does the target operate in sectors DOJ typically reviews (tech, defense, banking, telecom, agriculture)?
signals_in_document: Recitals or covenants specifically naming DOJ
Industry sector suggesting DOJ jurisdiction
Market share data or competitive overlap disclosure
Any reference to consent decree, divestiture, or behavioral remedy discussions

key_questions: Has DOJ issued a clearance letter?
Has a Second Request been issued?
Are divestiture or consent decree negotiations underway?
Has DOJ filed a complaint to block the transaction?
Has a consent decree been entered with the court?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Concurrent with HSR; Second Request adds 6–18+ months
monitoring.closing_trigger: DOJ clearance letter received, or consent decree entered and approved by court

approvals.name: FTC Clearance
approvals.description: FTC substantive antitrust review. FTC typically reviews pharma, healthcare, consumer goods, and retail sectors. May issue consent order, accept divestitures, or issue administrative complaint and seek preliminary injunction.

scoping.trigger_question: Is FTC the designated reviewing agency? Does the target operate in pharma, healthcare, consumer goods, retail, or technology sectors where FTC has primary jurisdiction?
signals_in_document: Recitals or covenants specifically naming FTC
Pharma, healthcare, consumer goods, or retail sector target
Any reference to consent order, FTC divestitures, or pull-and-refile

key_questions: Has FTC issued a clearance?
Has FTC requested a pull-and-refile of the HSR notification?
Has FTC issued an administrative complaint or sought a TRO?
Are consent order negotiations underway?
Has FTC voted to approve a consent order?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Concurrent with HSR waiting period
monitoring.closing_trigger: FTC clearance issued or consent order accepted

approvals.name: CFIUS Review
approvals.authority: Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (Treasury)
approvals.description: Interagency review under 50 U.S.C. § 4565 (FIRRMA). Mandatory declaration required for TID US businesses (critical technology, critical infrastructure, sensitive personal data) acquired by foreign persons. Voluntary notice available for any covered transaction.

scoping.trigger_question: Is any acquiror, co-investor, or financing party a non-US person or entity? Is the target a TID US business (critical technology, critical infrastructure, or sensitive personal data)? Is the target near a military installation (real estate rules)?
signals_in_document: Non-US acquiror nationality, domicile, or parent company
Sovereign wealth fund, state-owned enterprise, or government-affiliated investor
Target in technology, defense, semiconductor, AI, biotech, telecom, financial data, or critical infrastructure sectors
CFIUS referenced in regulatory approval covenants
Mandatory vs. voluntary filing election noted
Any reference to mitigation, NSA, or CFIUS conditions

mandatory_declaration_triggers: TID US business: critical technology (any % acquired by foreign person from covered countries)
TID US business: critical infrastructure (substantial interest acquired)
TID US business: sensitive personal data (substantial interest acquired)
threshold_details.note: Mandatory declaration must be filed 30 days before closing; voluntary notice 45-day review period

key_questions: Has a mandatory declaration or voluntary notice been filed with CFIUS?
Has CFIUS accepted the filing and started the review clock?
Has CFIUS issued a letter of assent (for declarations) or a written notice clearing the transaction?
Has CFIUS opened a 45-day investigation following the initial review?
Is a mitigation agreement (NSA or other) being negotiated?
Has CFIUS referred the transaction to the President for action?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Declaration: 30-day assessment; Voluntary notice: 45-day review + 45-day investigation if opened
monitoring.closing_trigger: CFIUS letter of assent or clearance notice received; or mitigation agreement executed

approvals.name: CFIUS Mitigation / National Security Agreement
approvals.authority: Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (Treasury)
approvals.description: National Security Agreement (NSA), Special Security Agreement (SSA), or other mitigation measures imposed as conditions to CFIUS approval. May include board observer restrictions, data security requirements, US-person-only access controls, government access rights.

scoping.trigger_question: Has CFIUS flagged national security concerns? Are mitigation measures referenced in the merger agreement covenants or conditions?
signals_in_document: Reference to 'mitigation,' 'NSA,' 'SSA,' or CFIUS conditions in covenants
Requirement to maintain US-person boards or security officers
Any obligation to maintain physical or logical separation of certain assets

key_questions: What specific mitigation terms has CFIUS proposed?
Has the NSA or other mitigation agreement been executed?
Have all closing conditions tied to CFIUS mitigation been satisfied?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Negotiated concurrently with CFIUS investigation; can add weeks to months
monitoring.closing_trigger: NSA or mitigation agreement fully executed and CFIUS clearance issued

approvals.name: FOCI Mitigation (DCSA)
approvals.description: Foreign Ownership, Control or Influence mitigation required when a cleared defense contractor (holding DoD facility security clearance) is acquired by a foreign person. May require proxy agreement, Special Security Agreement (SSA), or voting trust.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold a DoD Facility Security Clearance (FCL)? Does it perform work on classified government contracts?
signals_in_document: Target described as defense contractor with classified programs
Facility security clearance (FCL) referenced in representations
DCSA or DSS referenced in regulatory covenants
Any reference to classified contracts in the government contracts schedule

key_questions: Has DCSA been notified of the pending transaction?
Is a proxy agreement, SSA, or voting trust being established?
Have cleared personnel been identified for the board of directors under the arrangement?
Has DCSA approved the FOCI mitigation plan?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–12 months; highly deal-specific
monitoring.closing_trigger: DCSA approval of FOCI mitigation instrument

approvals.name: FCC License Transfer
approvals.description: FCC assignment or transfer of control applications required for any FCC-licensed facility or spectrum authorization. Covers broadcast (AM/FM/TV), wireless spectrum, MVPD, and common carrier telecom licenses. Applications filed on FCC Forms 314, 315, or 603.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold any FCC licenses: broadcast stations (AM/FM/TV), wireless spectrum (700 MHz, AWS, CBRS, mmWave), MVPD, common carrier telecom, or earth station licenses?
signals_in_document: Target described as broadcaster, wireless carrier, cable/MVPD, or telecom company
FCC licenses listed in intellectual property or regulatory representations
FCC approval specifically named in regulatory covenant
Team Telecom review referenced alongside FCC
Any foreign ownership above 25% threshold noted

threshold_details.foreign_ownership_limit: 25% direct, 50% indirect for common carrier licenses (Section 310(b))

key_questions: Have FCC assignment/transfer applications been filed for each license?
Has the FCC Media Bureau or Wireless Bureau placed applications on public notice?
Has the public comment/petition-to-deny period run?
Has FCC issued a grant order? Any conditions imposed?
Is Team Telecom conducting a parallel review due to foreign ownership?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 60–180 days for routine transfers; longer if petitions to deny filed or foreign ownership issues
monitoring.closing_trigger: FCC consent order issued; all licenses transferred

approvals.name: FERC Approval (Section 203)
approvals.description: FERC Section 203 authorization required for disposition of jurisdictional facilities (interstate natural gas pipelines, wholesale electric generation/transmission, hydropower). FERC applies a 'no adverse effect on competition, rates, or regulation' standard.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target own or operate FERC-jurisdictional assets: interstate natural gas pipelines (NGA Section 7), wholesale power generation or transmission (FPA Section 203), or FERC-licensed hydropower projects?
signals_in_document: Target described as electric utility, power generator, gas pipeline, or LDC
FERC approval referenced in regulatory covenants
Section 203 or Section 7 cited
PJM, MISO, CAISO, or other RTO membership referenced
FERC market-based rate authorization referenced in representations

key_questions: Has the FERC Section 203 application been filed?
Has FERC accepted the application and set a comment period?
Has FERC issued a deficiency letter requesting additional information?
Has FERC issued a consent order? Any conditions or behavioral commitments required?
Have related FERC filings been made (market-based rate amendments, reactive power studies)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–6 months for uncontested; 12+ months if contested or market power issues
monitoring.closing_trigger: FERC Section 203 authorization order issued

approvals.name: Federal Banking Regulators (FDIC / OCC / Federal Reserve)
approvals.authority: FDIC / OCC / Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve
approvals.description: Bank Merger Act approval required for mergers involving FDIC-insured institutions. OCC approves national bank mergers; FDIC approves state nonmember bank mergers; Federal Reserve approves bank holding company acquisitions under BHCA.

scoping.trigger_question: Is the target or acquiror a bank, savings institution, thrift, or bank holding company? Which federal regulator is primary (OCC for national banks; FDIC for state nonmember banks; Federal Reserve for BHCs)?
signals_in_document: Target described as bank, savings bank, thrift, or bank holding company
Federal Reserve, OCC, or FDIC specifically named in regulatory covenants
CRA rating referenced in representations
BHC Act or Bank Merger Act cited

key_questions: Have applications been filed with the appropriate primary federal regulator?
Has the public comment period run?
Any CRA concerns or community group opposition filed?
Has the Federal Reserve, OCC, or FDIC issued approval orders?
Are any state banking approvals also required?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–9 months
monitoring.closing_trigger: All applicable federal banking approvals received; state approvals also cleared

approvals.name: NRC License Transfer
approvals.description: NRC consent required for transfer of operating licenses for nuclear power plants and materials licenses. NRC conducts safety evaluation and antitrust review. Applications filed under 10 C.F.R. § 50.80.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target own or operate nuclear power plants or hold NRC materials licenses?
signals_in_document: Target described as nuclear power plant operator or owner
NRC referenced in regulatory approval covenants
Nuclear Operating License (COL or OL) listed in regulatory representations

key_questions: Have NRC license transfer applications been filed?
Has NRC published notice and opened the public comment period?
Is NRC conducting an antitrust review alongside the safety evaluation?
Has NRC issued a Safety Evaluation Report?
Has NRC issued a consent order for the license transfer?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 6–24 months
monitoring.closing_trigger: NRC consent order issued for each nuclear facility

approvals.name: SEC / FINRA Broker-Dealer & Investment Adviser
approvals.description: FINRA MC-400 membership continuance application required for change of control of broker-dealer. SEC Form ADV amendment required for registered investment adviser. Separate approval for futures commission merchants (CFTC/NFA).

scoping.trigger_question: Is the target a FINRA member broker-dealer or SEC-registered investment adviser?
signals_in_document: Target described as asset manager, broker-dealer, RIA, investment bank, or wealth manager
FINRA membership or SEC registration referenced in representations
FINRA MC-400 or Form ADV amendment referenced in regulatory covenants

key_questions: Has FINRA MC-400 application been filed?
Has FINRA issued a membership continuance approval?
Has Form ADV been amended to reflect the change of ownership?
Has SEC acknowledged the ADV amendment without further inquiry?
Are any state investment adviser registrations also requiring amendment?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 60–180 days for FINRA; Form ADV amendment effective upon filing
monitoring.closing_trigger: FINRA membership continuance approved; Form ADV updated

approvals.name: FDA / HHS / CMS Approvals
approvals.description: FDA marketing authorizations (NDA, BLA, 510(k)) transfer with the legal entity but manufacturing site changes require FDA notification. CMS Change of Ownership (CHOW) required for each Medicare/Medicaid provider number. May require state-level CHOW in parallel.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold FDA marketing authorizations, manufacturing registrations, or CMS provider numbers?
signals_in_document: Target in pharma, biotech, medical device, or healthcare services sector
FDA approvals, INDs, NDAs, BLAs, or 510(k)s listed in representations
Medicare/Medicaid provider numbers referenced
CMS CHOW obligation referenced in covenants

key_questions: Have CMS CHOW applications been filed for each provider number?
Has FDA been notified of any manufacturing site ownership changes?
Are any FDA supplement filings required for the transaction?
Have state CHOW applications been filed in parallel?
Have CMS provider agreements been accepted under new ownership?
monitoring.typical_timeline: CMS CHOW: 2–6 months per provider; FDA notifications: concurrent with closing
monitoring.closing_trigger: All CMS CHOW approvals received; FDA notifications filed

approvals.name: State PUC / PSC — Electric Utility
approvals.description: State public utility commission approval required in each state where the target holds an electric utility franchise. Standard is typically 'public interest' or 'no adverse effect on ratepayers.' Texas (PUCT/ERCOT) has separate process.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold electric utility franchises or distribution rights in any state? Which states?
signals_in_document: Target described as electric utility or electric distribution company (EDC)
State PUC approvals listed in regulatory covenants with specific state names
Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN) referenced in representations
Rate case history or rate base referenced

key_questions: Which specific states require PUC/PSC approval?
Have applications been filed in all required states?
Which states have opened evidentiary hearings?
Any state public advocate or consumer counsel opposing?
Which states have issued approval orders? Any conditions imposed (rate freezes, capital commitments)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 6–24 months per state; longest state is usually critical path
monitoring.closing_trigger: All required state PUC approvals received

approvals.name: State PUC / PSC — Natural Gas Utility
approvals.description: State PUC approval required for change of control of local gas distribution companies (LDCs) in each franchise state.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target own or operate local natural gas distribution companies (LDCs) or gas utility franchises?
signals_in_document: Target described as natural gas utility or LDC
State gas PUC approvals listed
Gas distribution franchise referenced in representations

key_questions: Which states require gas PUC approval?
Have applications been filed in all required states?
Any state with evidentiary hearing or contested proceeding?
What conditions are being proposed in each state?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 6–18 months per state
monitoring.closing_trigger: All required state gas PUC approvals received

approvals.name: State PUC — Telecom (CLEC/ILEC)
approvals.description: State PUC approval required for transfer of CLEC or ILEC certificates of authority in states where the target holds them. Most active in states with significant landline or regulated telecom operations.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold state certificates of authority as a CLEC or ILEC? In which states?
signals_in_document: Target described as CLEC, ILEC, or telecom carrier
State telecom PUC approvals referenced
Local exchange certificates listed in regulatory representations

key_questions: Which states require telecom PUC approval?
Have applications been filed in all relevant states?
Any state ordering conditions (build-out commitments, service quality requirements)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–12 months per state
monitoring.closing_trigger: All state telecom transfer approvals received

approvals.name: State Insurance Commissioner — Form A (Domicile State)
approvals.authority: State Department of Insurance — Domicile State
approvals.description: Acquisition-of-control filing (Form A) required in the target insurer's state of domicile under the Insurance Holding Company System Regulatory Act. Typically requires a public hearing and 60-day review period.

scoping.trigger_question: Is the target or any target subsidiary an insurance company? What is the state of domicile?
signals_in_document: Target described as insurer, reinsurer, or insurance holding company
State of domicile identified in representations
Form A filing obligation referenced in covenants
Insurance holding company act or Model Act cited

threshold_details.ownership_threshold: 10% acquisition of voting securities triggers Form A in most states

key_questions: Has Form A been filed in the domicile state?
Has the Insurance Commissioner scheduled a public hearing?
Has the commissioner retained an independent financial examiner or actuary?
Has the commissioner issued an order approving or disapproving the acquisition?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 60–180 days from filing
monitoring.closing_trigger: Domicile state Insurance Commissioner approval order issued

approvals.name: State Insurance Commissioner — Form E (Non-Domicile States)
approvals.authority: State Departments of Insurance — All Licensed States
approvals.description: Pre-acquisition notification (Form E) required in all states (other than domicile) where the target insurer holds a certificate of authority. Form E is notice-based but some states have review rights. Can be a 50-state process for national carriers.

scoping.trigger_question: In how many states does the target insurance company hold certificates of authority? Form E must be filed in each non-domicile licensed state.
signals_in_document: Target is a national or multi-state insurer
Insurance licensing schedule identifies all licensed states
Form E or multi-state filing obligation referenced in covenants

key_questions: In which non-domicile states has Form E been filed?
Have all states acknowledged receipt?
Has any non-domicile state asserted review rights or raised concerns?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 30 days notice period per state; typically run in parallel
monitoring.closing_trigger: Form E filings complete in all licensed states; no state objection unresolved

approvals.name: State AG Antitrust Review
approvals.description: State attorneys general can independently investigate and challenge mergers under state antitrust law. Can seek injunctions in state or federal court. Most active AGs: California (DOJ), New York (AG), Texas (AG), Illinois (AG), Colorado (AG), Massachusetts (AG).

scoping.trigger_question: Does the deal have significant geographic concentration or consumer-facing market presence in large states with active AG offices? Has any state AG issued an HSR access letter?
signals_in_document: Geographic revenue concentration in CA, NY, TX, IL, CO referenced
Consumer-facing business with retail, healthcare, or media exposure
Any reference to state AG inquiry, CID, or multistate investigation in disclosure schedules
Outside date extended to allow state AG review

key_questions: Have any state AGs issued Civil Investigative Demands (CIDs)?
Are any states participating in a multistate AG working group?
Has any AG filed a lawsuit or motion for preliminary injunction?
Have any states entered into consent agreements alongside or separate from federal agencies?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Concurrent with federal review; state litigation can continue post-federal clearance
monitoring.closing_trigger: No pending state AG challenge; any state consent agreements executed

approvals.name: State Pre-Merger Notification
approvals.authority: State AG Offices (CA, NY, and others)
approvals.description: Independent state pre-merger filing requirements beyond federal HSR. California (Cal. Corp. Code § 1110) and New York (GBL § 343) have their own requirements, as do several other states.

scoping.trigger_question: Do the parties have sufficient California or New York nexus to trigger state-level pre-merger notification? Are there other state-specific filing requirements?
signals_in_document: California or New York revenues or assets above state thresholds
State pre-merger notification referenced in regulatory covenants

key_questions: Have state pre-merger notifications been filed in all applicable states?
Have applicable waiting periods elapsed?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Concurrent with HSR
monitoring.closing_trigger: All state pre-merger waiting periods elapsed

approvals.name: State CON / COPA (Hospital & Healthcare)
approvals.description: Certificate of Need (CON) required in ~35 states for hospital acquisitions and capital expenditures. Certificate of Public Advantage (COPA) available in some states to allow otherwise anticompetitive hospital mergers with regulatory oversight.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target operate licensed hospitals or inpatient facilities in CON states? Is a COPA being sought as an alternative to antitrust clearance?
signals_in_document: Target described as hospital system or health system
CON or COPA referenced in regulatory covenants
Specific CON states listed in approval schedule

key_questions: Have CON applications been filed in all required states?
Has any state health department scheduled a public evidentiary hearing?
Is a COPA being pursued in any state as an antitrust shield?
Have CON approvals been issued? Any conditions (charity care, service commitments)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–18 months per state
monitoring.closing_trigger: All required CON approvals received

approvals.name: State Gaming Commission Approvals
approvals.description: Gaming license change of control applications required in each state where the target holds casino, gaming device, or lottery concession licenses. Each state has its own suitability review and background investigation requirements.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold gaming licenses in any state? Which states?
signals_in_document: Target described as casino operator, gaming company, or lottery concessionaire
State gaming license schedule in representations
Gaming commission approvals referenced in regulatory covenants

key_questions: Have change-of-control applications been filed with each state gaming commission?
Has suitability review / background investigation been initiated for acquiror principals?
Which states have completed review and issued approval?
Any state imposing conditions (local hiring, capital investment commitments)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–24 months; Nevada and New Jersey most intensive
monitoring.closing_trigger: All required state gaming approvals received

approvals.name: State Cannabis Regulators
approvals.description: Cannabis license transfer or change of control approval required in each state where the target holds cannabis licenses (cultivation, manufacturing, dispensary). Often requires local approval in addition to state approval.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold cannabis licenses in any state? Which states and license types?
signals_in_document: Target described as cannabis operator, MSO, dispensary chain, or grower
Cannabis license schedule in representations
State cannabis regulator approvals referenced in covenants
Local approval obligations noted

key_questions: Have license transfer applications been filed in each applicable state?
Are any states under a moratorium on license transfers?
Have local approvals been obtained where required?
Which states have completed transfer approvals?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–12 months per state
monitoring.closing_trigger: All required state cannabis transfer approvals received

approvals.name: EU — European Commission Merger Review (Phase I / II)
approvals.description: EC merger review under EU Merger Regulation (EUMR) 139/2004. One-stop-shop for EU-wide transactions. Phase I: 25 working days. Phase II: additional 90 working days. Can be extended for remedies. Member state referrals possible under Article 9 (to national CA) or Article 22 (from national CAs to EC).

scoping.trigger_question: Do the parties have combined worldwide turnover exceeding €5 billion AND each of at least two parties has EU-wide turnover exceeding €250 million? Or do they meet the alternative two-thirds rule thresholds?
signals_in_document: EC merger filing referenced in regulatory covenants
EU revenues of each party disclosed
One-stop-shop analysis referenced; member state filings waived
Outside date allowing 18–24 months for EC process

key_questions: Has the Form CO (or short-form CO) been submitted to DG Comp?
Has the Phase I 25-working-day clock started?
Has EC issued a Phase I clearance decision?
Has EC opened a Phase II investigation? What are the theories of harm?
Has EC issued a Statement of Objections?
What remedies are being discussed? Structural (divestitures) vs. behavioral?
What is the current Phase II deadline (potentially extended for remedies)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Phase I: 25 working days (~5 weeks); Phase II: 90 additional working days (~18 weeks); extensions possible
monitoring.closing_trigger: EC clearance decision issued (Phase I or Phase II)

approvals.name: UK — CMA Merger Review
approvals.description: Post-Brexit, UK merger control is entirely separate from EU. Mandatory filing if UK turnover of target exceeds £70M OR combined share of supply in UK exceeds 25%. CMA has broad jurisdiction to investigate even below thresholds. Phase 1: 40 working days. Phase 2: 24 weeks.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target have UK turnover above £70M? Or would the combined entity have a 25%+ share of supply in any UK market?
signals_in_document: UK revenues of target disclosed
CMA filing referenced in regulatory covenants
UK market share data noted in competitive analysis
Parallel EU and UK filing obligations both referenced

key_questions: Has a voluntary or mandatory notification been made to CMA?
Has CMA opened a Phase 1 investigation?
Has CMA issued an Enforcement Order (hold-separate)?
Has CMA cleared in Phase 1 or referred to Phase 2?
Is a Phase 2 inquiry group appointed?
Has CMA issued provisional or final findings?
Any remedies being negotiated (UILs in Phase 1 or remedies order in Phase 2)?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Phase 1: 40 working days; Phase 2: 24 weeks
monitoring.closing_trigger: CMA Phase 1 clearance or Phase 2 final report with no adverse findings

approvals.name: UK — National Security and Investment Act (NSIA)
approvals.description: Mandatory notification for acquisitions of 25%+ of shares/voting rights in entities operating in 17 sensitive sectors. Voluntary notification available for other transactions. The ISU can call in any transaction. UK NSIA is separate from and parallel to CMA review.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target carry on activities in any of the 17 NSIA mandatory sectors? Is the acquiror acquiring 25%+ of shares or voting rights?
signals_in_document: UK NSIA or Investment Security Unit referenced in regulatory covenants

Target operates in any NSIA sector: advanced materials, AI, civil nuclear, communications, computing hardware, critical suppliers (defence/emergency services), cryptographic authentication, data infrastructure, defence, energy, military and dual-use, quantum technologies, robotics, satellites and space, suppliers to emergency services, synthetic biology, transport

threshold_details.trigger_threshold: 25% or more of shares or voting rights

key_questions: Has an NSIA mandatory or voluntary notification been filed with the Investment Security Unit?
Has the ISU issued a call-in notice?
Has the ISU cleared the transaction or issued a final order?
Are any conditions (asset access restrictions, board composition) being imposed?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 30-day initial review; 30-day assessment if called in; 45-day further review possible
monitoring.closing_trigger: ISU clearance notice received or mandatory period elapsed without call-in

approvals.name: China — SAMR Merger Filing
approvals.description: Suspensory pre-merger notification to SAMR required above thresholds. Three-phase review: Phase I (30 days), Phase II (90 days), Phase III (60 days). SAMR has historically been a significant approval for global deals with China operations.

scoping.trigger_question: Do the combined parties have global revenues exceeding RMB 10 billion AND each of at least two parties has China revenues exceeding RMB 400 million?
signals_in_document: China revenues disclosed by each party
SAMR filing referenced in regulatory covenants
China nexus for both parties confirmed
Outside date set to accommodate SAMR timeline

key_questions: Has the SAMR pre-merger filing been accepted?
Is SAMR in Phase I (30 days), Phase II (90 days), or Phase III extended review (60 days)?
Has SAMR requested additional information or documentation?
Has SAMR proposed conditions (structural remedies, behavioral commitments)?
Has SAMR issued an unconditional or conditional approval decision?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Phase I: 30 days; Phase II: 90 days; Phase III: 60 days; total max ~180 days
monitoring.closing_trigger: SAMR unconditional or conditional approval decision issued

approvals.name: Canada — Competition Bureau Pre-Merger Notification
approvals.description: Pre-merger notification required under the Competition Act if size-of-parties and size-of-transaction thresholds are met. 30-day initial waiting period. Bureau may issue Supplementary Information Request (SIR), adding 30 days after compliance.

scoping.trigger_question: Do the parties have combined Canadian assets or revenues (in Canada) exceeding CAD $400 million AND the transaction value exceeds CAD $93 million (indexed)?
signals_in_document: Canadian revenues or assets of each party disclosed
Competition Bureau filing referenced in covenants
ICA review referenced alongside Competition Bureau

key_questions: Has pre-merger notification been filed with the Competition Bureau?
Has the 30-day initial waiting period started?
Has the Bureau issued a Supplementary Information Request (SIR)?
Has the Bureau issued a No-Action Letter?
Has the Bureau issued a Consent Agreement or applied to the Competition Tribunal?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 30 days initial; SIR adds 30 days after compliance
monitoring.closing_trigger: No-Action Letter received or waiting period expired

approvals.name: Canada — Investment Canada Act (ICA)
approvals.authority: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED)
approvals.description: Two-stage review: (1) Net Benefit review for large direct acquisitions by WTO investors (threshold ~CAD $1.287B for non-SOE; lower for SOE and cultural industries); (2) National Security review applies to any foreign investment regardless of size.

scoping.trigger_question: Is the acquiror non-Canadian? Does the transaction meet the ICA review threshold? Does the target operate in a sensitive sector triggering national security review?
signals_in_document: Acquiror nationality and ICA threshold analysis referenced
ICA national security or net benefit review referenced in regulatory covenants
Canadian assets or enterprise value referenced for threshold determination
Target sector sensitivity (defence, critical minerals, AI, health data)

key_questions: Has an ICA application or notification been filed?
Has the Minister issued a net benefit approval?
Has a national security review been ordered?
Has the Governor in Council issued a national security decision?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Net benefit: 45 days initial + 30-day extensions; National security: up to 200 days
monitoring.closing_trigger: ICA approval received; no national security order

approvals.name: Brazil — CADE Pre-Merger Filing
approvals.description: Suspensory pre-merger notification required. CADE's Superintendência-Geral (SG) conducts initial review; referred to Tribunal for complex cases. Fast-track (30 days) for non-horizontal transactions; ordinary review up to 240 days.

scoping.trigger_question: Does at least one party have Brazil revenues above BRL 750 million AND at least one other party have Brazil revenues above BRL 75 million?
signals_in_document: Brazil revenues of each party disclosed
CADE filing referenced in regulatory covenants
Suspensory nature — no closing until CADE approval

key_questions: Has the CADE pre-merger filing been submitted and accepted?
Is CADE using fast-track (30 days) or ordinary review (up to 240 days)?
Has CADE's SG referred the case to the Tribunal?
What remedies or conditions are being proposed?
Has CADE issued a final approval decision?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Fast-track: 30 days; Ordinary: up to 240 days
monitoring.closing_trigger: CADE approval decision (unconditional or conditional)

approvals.name: Germany — Bundeskartellamt (BKartA)
approvals.description: Pre-merger notification required if German thresholds met independently of EC jurisdiction. Phase I: 1 month. Phase II: 4 months. BKartA has strong consumer welfare and market structure focus.

scoping.trigger_question: Do the parties have combined worldwide revenues exceeding €500 million AND at least one party with Germany revenues exceeding €25 million AND at least one other party with Germany revenues exceeding €5 million? Is EC jurisdiction absent (two-thirds rule applies)?
signals_in_document: German revenues disclosed
BKartA filing referenced in covenants
EC one-stop-shop analysis — if EC has jurisdiction, BKartA generally does not

threshold_details.note: Also applies regardless of revenue if transaction value > €400M and target has significant German operations

key_questions: Has BKartA filing been submitted?
Is BKartA in Phase I (1 month) or Phase II (4 months)?
Has BKartA issued a Statement of Objections?
Are structural or behavioral remedies being negotiated?
Has BKartA issued a clearance decision?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Phase I: 1 month; Phase II: 4 months
monitoring.closing_trigger: BKartA clearance decision issued

approvals.name: Target Shareholder Vote
approvals.description: Stockholder approval required in most public company mergers. Threshold depends on merger structure and state of incorporation (typically majority of shares outstanding for Delaware long-form mergers).

scoping.trigger_question: Is the target a public company? What vote threshold is required under the merger agreement and applicable state law? Is any controlling stockholder providing a written consent?
signals_in_document: Stockholder approval condition in merger agreement
Record date and meeting date obligations
Required vote threshold specified (majority of outstanding shares, or supermajority)
No-shop and force-the-vote provisions
Any written consent in lieu of meeting (controlling stockholder)

threshold_details.typical_threshold: Majority of shares outstanding (Delaware); check target charter for supermajority

key_questions: Has the proxy statement / definitive proxy been filed and mailed?
Has SEC declared the registration statement effective (if S-4)?
Has a record date been set?
What is the meeting date?
What is the preliminary proxy vote count?
Have any significant stockholders announced their voting intention (for/against)?
Has any stockholder filed an appraisal demand?
monitoring.typical_timeline: SEC proxy review: 30 days; stockholder meeting: 60–90 days after effectiveness
monitoring.closing_trigger: Stockholder vote obtained at duly convened meeting

approvals.name: SEC Proxy / S-4 Registration Statement
approvals.description: Form S-4 registration statement required for stock-for-stock mergers; proxy statement (Schedule 14A) required for cash mergers requiring target stockholder vote. SEC staff review typically takes 30 days; comments must be resolved before effectiveness.

scoping.trigger_question: Is the acquiror issuing registered shares as merger consideration (requiring S-4)? Or is this a cash deal requiring only a proxy statement?
signals_in_document: Stock consideration referenced (S-4 required)
Cash consideration with target shareholder vote (proxy required)
SEC filing obligation and effectiveness condition referenced in covenants

key_questions: Has the S-4 or preliminary proxy been filed with the SEC?
Has SEC issued comments? Have comments been resolved?
Has the S-4 been declared effective by the SEC?
Has the definitive proxy been mailed to stockholders?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 30 days for initial SEC review; 10 days for second review; effectiveness within days after final comment resolution
monitoring.closing_trigger: S-4 declared effective or definitive proxy filed and mailed

approvals.name: Debt / Indenture Change of Control Consents
approvals.description: Credit agreement change of control provisions may require consent or trigger repayment obligations. Bond indentures typically contain change of control put rights (repurchase at 101%) rather than consent requirements. Lender consents may be needed for assumption of credit facilities.

scoping.trigger_question: Do the target's credit facilities or indentures contain change of control provisions triggered by the merger? Are there repayment obligations, consent requirements, or put rights?
signals_in_document: Target debt schedule and credit agreement/indenture representations
Change of control definition in listed debt instruments
Debt assumption or refinancing covenant in merger agreement
Consent solicitation or tender offer for notes referenced

key_questions: Have consent solicitation materials been distributed to lenders/noteholders?
What is the required consent threshold and what has been obtained?
Is a tender offer for notes being launched alongside the consent solicitation?
Has the acquiror arranged replacement financing?
Have all required lender consents been obtained?
monitoring.typical_timeline: Consent solicitations typically run 2–4 weeks
monitoring.closing_trigger: Required debt consents obtained or refinancing arranged

approvals.name: Government Contract Novation (FAR Part 42)
approvals.description: FAR Part 42.12 novation agreement required for each federal prime contract when the government contractor is acquired (assets or stock). Must be submitted to each cognizant contracting officer with supporting documentation. Assignment without novation is void.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target have US federal prime contracts? Are novation agreements required (vs. change of name agreements)?
signals_in_document: Target described as government contractor
List of material government contracts in representations
FAR novation obligation referenced in covenants
Any CPSR (Contractor Purchasing System Review) or consent to subcontract obligations noted

threshold_details.note: Asset deals require novation; stock deals may require change-of-name agreements only for certain contracts

key_questions: Has a list of prime contracts requiring novation been compiled?
Have novation applications been submitted to each contracting officer?
Which agencies have executed novation agreements?
Are any classified contract novations requiring DCSA involvement?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 3–12 months; varies by agency and contract complexity
monitoring.closing_trigger: All required novation agreements executed by contracting agencies

approvals.name: ITAR / EAR License Transfer (DDTC / BIS)
approvals.authority: Directorate of Defense Trade Controls (State) / Bureau of Industry and Security (Commerce)
approvals.description: ITAR-controlled defense articles (22 C.F.R. Parts 120–130) require DDTC notification of change of ownership. EAR-controlled dual-use items (15 C.F.R. Parts 730–774) require BIS review of export license transfers. Commodity Jurisdiction determinations may be needed.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target manufacture, export, or broker ITAR-controlled defense articles or EAR dual-use items? Does the target hold active export licenses from DDTC or BIS?
signals_in_document: Target described as defense contractor, aerospace company, or advanced technology company
ITAR compliance program or export control section referenced in representations
DDTC registration or active export licenses listed
Commodity Jurisdiction determinations referenced

key_questions: Has DDTC been notified of the pending change of control?
Have active ITAR export licenses been transferred or amended?
Have BIS export licenses been transferred?
Are any Commodity Jurisdiction determinations needed post-closing?
Have ITAR Technical Assistance Agreements (TAAs) been amended?
monitoring.typical_timeline: DDTC notification: concurrent with closing; license amendments: 60–180 days
monitoring.closing_trigger: DDTC notification submitted; material export licenses transferred or amended

approvals.name: DEA Registration Transfer
approvals.description: DEA controlled substance registrations cannot be assigned or transferred automatically. Each DEA registration number requires a separate transfer of registration application. Applies to manufacturers, distributors, researchers, and dispensers of Schedule I–V substances.

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold DEA registrations for manufacturing, distribution, or dispensing of controlled substances?
signals_in_document: Target in pharma, specialty pharma, or healthcare distribution sector
DEA registrations listed in representations
DEA transfer obligation referenced in covenants

key_questions: Have DEA transfer of registration applications been filed for each registration number?
Has DEA acknowledged and processed transfers?
Can the acquiror operate under existing DEA registrations during the transfer process?
monitoring.typical_timeline: 30–90 days per registration
monitoring.closing_trigger: All DEA registration transfers approved

approvals.name: DCSA Facility Security Clearance (FCL)
approvals.description: DCSA must be notified of any transaction that would result in a change of ownership of a cleared defense contractor. Classified contracts cannot proceed without maintaining FCL. May require FOCI mitigation (see separate entry).

scoping.trigger_question: Does the target hold a DCSA facility security clearance (FCL) and perform work on classified programs?
signals_in_document: Target described as cleared defense contractor
FCL or classified program participation referenced in representations
DCSA or FOCI mitigation referenced in covenants

key_questions: Has DCSA been notified of the pending transaction?
Will the FCL be maintained, surrendered, or transferred?
If foreign buyer, is FOCI mitigation required? (See foci_dcsa entry)
monitoring.typical_timeline: Concurrent with closing notification
monitoring.closing_trigger: DCSA notification accepted; FCL continuity confirmed
